newsSince: No genuinely new fundamental catalyst since June 9; the OpenAI 6GW/Oracle 50k-GPU deals and MI400/MI450 ramp (with Meta committing up to 6GW and MI450 customer forecasts exceeding expectations) were already in the prior case. Stock +9.6% since June 9 (+133% YTD) on continued AI-deal momentum and sector flow, not node-specific news.

sources.label: S&P Global: AMD next-gen AI chips 2026

verdicts.rankRationale: AI-node bottleneck lens unchanged since June 9: second-source MI400/OpenAI ramp intact, no fresh node-specific evidence. The +9.6% rebound is sector flow that erodes the entry-asymmetry the prior case credited; at $857B and +133% YTD it stays the crowded Nvidia-alternative. Hold.
verdicts.bull: MI450/Helios 2H26 ramp plus OpenAI 6GW deal gives durable second-source accelerator demand.
verdicts.neutral: Roadmap known since CES; recent move is flow, thesis flat vs June 9.
verdicts.bear: Most-owned Nvidia alternative; software/platform gap and margin conversion still unproven at $857B cap.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: MI450/Helios 2H26 production ramp and hyperscaler design wins.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowding and ROCm software gap limiting margin capture.

verdicts.rankRationale: CPO lens: AMD has scale-up fabric/SerDes relevance but no direct co-packaged-optics monetization, and nothing changed on that front since June 9. Indirect beneficiary only; rank 85 already reflects the weaker CPO linkage. Hold.
verdicts.bull: Open scale-up fabric (UALink/Ethernet) adoption pulls AMD into optical interconnect demand over time.
verdicts.neutral: No direct CPO catalyst; AMD remains a derivative play on this page.
verdicts.bear: Network/optics names monetize CPO far more directly than AMD.
verdicts.primaryRisk: CPO value accrues to optics/switch vendors, not the accelerator.

verdicts.rankRationale: Master under-owned-bottleneck list: AMD is the opposite of under-owned at $857B / +133% YTD with consensus accelerator demand. No new bottleneck-scarcity evidence since June 9; the +9.6% bounce reverses some of the asymmetry the prior hold credited. Stays low on this list.
verdicts.bull: Genuine second-source accelerator scarcity if MI400 ramps cleanly.
verdicts.neutral: Thesis intact but heavily owned; alpha score rewards scarcity AMD lacks.
verdicts.bear: Cap concentration and crowding cap residual alpha on this lens.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: MI400 supply ramp converting into share gains.
